channel.title: Priya Darshani Essays
channel.description: Essays by Priya Darshani on AI validation, enterprise deployment, governance, trust, and human judgement.

item.title: Beyond Benchmarks: Why AI Needs Better Evidence Before Deployment
item.description: Benchmarks tell us what AI can do. Organisations need to know whether to trust it. That distinction is becoming one of the most consequential questions in enterprise AI.

item.title: The Missing Infrastructure for AI
item.description: Why measuring models is no longer enough, and why the future of AI depends on better ways of measuring human judgement, trust and deployment readiness.

item.title: Why Regulated Industries Cannot Treat AI Validation as a Checkbox
item.description: In regulated environments, the cost of a poorly deployed AI system is not just operational. It is reputational, legal, and deeply human. That changes what deployment readiness actually requires.

item.title: The Difference Between AI Safety, AI Security, and AI Trust
item.description: Strategy, AI validation, behavioural science, enterprise deployment, governance, and trust. These are the six lenses I keep returning to.

item.title: Agentic AI Needs Validation Infrastructure, Not Just Governance Documents
item.description: The honest version of AI validation starts with uncomfortable questions. What can fail, who notices, and what happens next?

item.title: What Enterprises Should Measure Before Deploying AI Agents
item.description: Validation is not a pass-or-fail stamp. It is a structured way to understand performance, limits, risk, and readiness across real use cases.

item.title: Why Human Judgment Still Matters in AI Evaluation
item.description: Automated benchmarks are useful. They cannot tell you whether an AI system behaves well inside the messy reality of enterprise work.

item.title: From AI Demo to Deployment: What Readiness Actually Requires
item.description: Most enterprises do not have an AI problem. They have a trust problem. The gap between what AI systems can do and what organisations are prepared to deploy is widening, and the cost of that gap is real.

item.title: The AI Trust Gap: Why Capability Is Moving Faster Than Confidence
item.description: AI validation is not a technical checkpoint. It is the discipline that decides whether an organisation can trust a system enough to put it in front of customers, employees, and regulators.
